channel.title: Indian Defence Research Wing
channel.description: INDIAN DEFENCE NEWS, LATEST DEFENCE NEWS, INDIAN ARMY, INDIAN AIR FORCE, INDIAN NAVY, DEFENSE NEWS

item.title: India’s S5 SSBN Program Gains Momentum with Twin Submarine Plan and 190 MW Reactor for K-6 ICBM Capability
item.description: India’s next-generation nuclear submarine program, centered on the S5-class SSBN, is reportedly gathering pace with indications that a second submarine may soon be sanctioned alongside the lead boat currently under construction. According to sources, the program—kept under strict confidentiality—has already crossed a critical milestone, with the first steel cutting or formal laying down of the lead S5 submarine taking place in the closing weeks of 2025.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: India Unveils Plans to develop a Soft-Kill Laser Defence Shield to Defeat Laser-Guided Bombs
item.description: India’s Defence Research and Development Organisation (DRDO) is advancing a critical soft-kill capability with the development of the Laser Threat Detection & Decoy System (LTDDS), designed to protect high-value assets from precision airstrikes involving laser-guided munitions.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: Dhruv ALH Crosses 440 Units, HAL Eyes 10 More Years of Production With NG Variant Boost
item.description: Hindustan Aeronautics Limited’s (HAL) Dhruv Advanced Light Helicopter (ALH) program is set to remain in active production well into the mid-2030s, underpinned by a robust order pipeline, continuous upgrades, and the introduction of the Dhruv NG variant. Industry assessments and HAL’s financial disclosures indicate that the production line will stay operational for at least the next 8–10 years, ensuring sustained output and long-term support for one of India’s most widely deployed rotary platforms.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: Ghatak Reborn: India’s 39,000 Crore Stealth UCAV Program and the Rise of Autonomous Strike Warfare
item.description: India’s Ghatak program, now restructured under the Remotely Piloted Strike Aircraft (RPSA) initiative, represents one of the most ambitious efforts to redefine the country’s airpower doctrine. With an estimated investment of ?39,000 crore and a clear roadmap for operational induction, the program has transitioned from a technology demonstrator to a full-scale acquisition effort.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: Su-30MKI SB050 Incident Revisited: From Mid-Air Ejection Mystery to Ground Training Asset for IAF Crews
item.description: The October 14, 2014 incident involving Su-30MKI tail number SB050 remains one of the most debated safety cases in Indian Air Force history, not only because of the unusual sequence of events but also due to what followed afterward. Piloted by Flying Officer Anoop Kumar and Wing Commander Siddharth Vishwas Munje, the aircraft was on final approach to Lohegaon Airbase with landing configuration properly set—flaps deployed and airspeed stabilized—when both pilots were suddenly forced to eject. Crucially, subsequent investigation revealed that the ejection seats had activated without manual input from the crew, pointing toward a technical malfunction rather than pilot-initiated action.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: DRDO’s Stratospheric Airship: India’s Emerging High Altitude Pseudo Satellite (HAPS) for Unmatched Persistent Surveillance
item.description: The Defence Research and Development Organisation (DRDO) is advancing a cutting-edge stratospheric airship programme, widely recognised as a High Altitude Pseudo Satellite (HAPS), to deliver persistent, cost-effective intelligence gathering far above conventional threats. Highly placed sources confirm that this unmanned platform will operate at altitudes between 45,000 and 60,000 feet (approximately 14 to 18 kilometres), placing it well beyond the reach of most short-range and many medium-range surface-to-air missiles.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: Geopolitical Risks Cast Shadow Over Pakistan’s JF-17 Fighter Jet Exports
item.description: Despite its competitive pricing and joint development with China, the JF-17 Thunder fighter jet is facing unexpected headwinds in the global export market. Potential buyers are increasingly hesitant to commit to deals with Pakistan, citing deep concerns over the long-term reliability of supply chains tied to the aircraft’s primary assembly hub.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: Clean Energy Milestone: Kudankulam Unit-3 Moves Closer to Commissioning
item.description: A significant stride in India’s journey towards energy security and sustainability! Nuclear Power Corporation of India Limited (NPCIL) has achieved a major commissioning milestone at Kudankulam Nuclear Power Project, Unit-3 with the successful commencement of “Spillage to Open Reactor” on 25 April 2026. This critical activity ensures flushing and conditioning of safety systems and main coolant pipelines using light water, establishing the required cleanliness for advanced commissioning stages.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: India poised to become 2nd country after Russia to operate fast breeder reactor
item.description: India is poised to become the second country after Russia to operate a commercial-level fast breeder reactor as the indigenously designed 500 MWe Prototype Fast Breeder Reactor (PFBR) at Kalpakkam in Tamil Nadu achieved first criticality on April 6, Union Minister Jitendra Singh said on Monday. Addressing a workshop of MPs and MLAs on “Small Modular Reactors”, the minister said, this reactor, developed by the Indira Gandhi Centre for Atomic Research (IGCAR) and built by BHAVINI, marks the start of the second stage of India’s three-stage nuclear power programme, using uranium-plutonium mixed oxide fuel to produce more fuel than it consumes.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: ‘Sigma Advanced Systems’ secures 300 Million Pound longterm agreement with Rolls-Royce
item.description: Sigma Advanced Systems, a globally integrated aerospace and defence company, on Monday said it has signed a seven-year long-term agreement with Rolls-Royce, marking a significant milestone in its global aerospace journey. Valued at nearly 300 million Pound (approximately INR 3,800 crore), the agreement strengthens strategic relationship between the two companies and positions Sigma Advanced Systems at the core of critical global engineering programmes.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: NSA Ajit Doval discusses regional situation with UAE Prez Zayed Al Nahyan
item.description: National Security Advisor Ajit Doval on Sunday met UAE President Mohamed bin Zayed Al Nahyan and discussed measures to deepen the comprehensive strategic partnership and the regional situation. The Indian Embassy in the UAE said Doval conveyed Prime Minister Narendra Modi’s greetings to the UAE leader during his official visit to the Arab country. “Measures to deepen Comprehensive Strategic Partnership, the regional situation, and other issues of mutual interest were discussed,” the Indian Embassy said in the post on X.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: Hafiz Saeed’s close aide and key LeT figure Sheikh Yousaf Afridi shot dead in Pakistan
item.description: Sheikh Yousaf Afridi, a key Lashkar-e-Taiba (LeT) figure and a close associate of the Mumbai terror attack mastermind Hafiz Saeed, was shot dead by unidentified gunmen in Pakistan’s Khyber Pakhtunkhwa, police said on Monday.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: Rajnath Singh arrives in Bishkek to attend SCO Defence Ministers’ Meeting
item.description: Defence Minister Rajnath Singh on Monday arrived in Kyrgyzstan’s Bishkek to attend the Shanghai Cooperation Organisation (SCO) Defence Ministers’ Meeting. “On his arrival at Bishkek to attend Meeting of Ministers of Defence of the SCO Member States from 27–28 April 2026, Hon’ble Raksha Mantri Shri Rajnath Singh ji was welcomed by Ambassador Singh at Manas airport on 27 April 2026,” the Indian Embassy in Bishkek posted on X.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: INS Sudarshini concludes port call at Canary Islands
item.description: The Indian Navy’s Sail Training Ship, INS Sudarshini, concluded a landmark three-day port call at Las Palmas, Canary Islands, marking a pivotal milestone in its Lokayan 26 transoceanic expedition, the Ministry of Defence stated on Monday.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: Karnataka: Mangaluru cooker blast accused sentenced to 10 years jail
item.description: A special National Investigation Agency (NIA) court on Monday sentenced terror accused Mohammed Shariq to 10 years of rigorous imprisonment in connection with the Mangaluru cooker blast case. Shariq had pleaded guilty before the court during the trial.
item.dc:creator: admin
